contents.value:
```json
{"name": "value", "count": 42}
```
A JSON **object** is an unordered set of key-value pairs enclosed in `{}`. Keys must be double-quoted strings. Pairs are separated by commas.

```json
[1, "two", true, null]
```
A JSON **array** is an ordered list of values enclosed in `[]`. Values are separated by commas and can be any JSON type.

```json
"Hello, World!"
```
**String** - A sequence of Unicode characters wrapped in double quotes. Supports escape sequences: `\n` (newline), `\t` (tab), `\"` (quote), `\uXXXX` (unicode).

```json
42
3.14
1.5e10
```
**Number** - Integer or floating-point. No leading zeros, no hex/octal notation. Supports negative numbers and scientific notation (e/E).

```json
true
false
```
**Boolean** - Represents a logical true or false value. Must be all lowercase.

```json
null
```
**null** - Represents the absence of a value. Always lowercase. Commonly used for optional/missing fields.

```json
"enabled": true
```
**true** - Boolean literal representing a truthy value. Must be lowercase.

```json
"enabled": false
```
**false** - Boolean literal representing a falsy value. Must be lowercase.

```
\"  double quote
\\  backslash
\/  forward slash
\n  newline
\t  tab
\r  carriage return
\uXXXX  unicode
```
**Escape sequences** in JSON strings. The backslash `\` is the escape character.

```json
{"$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema"}
```
**JSON Schema** provides a way to validate the structure of JSON data. The `$schema` keyword declares the schema version.

An **HTML element** consists of a start tag, content, and end tag.
```html
<tag>content</tag>
```

An **attribute** provides additional information about an element.
```html
<tag attr="value">
```

A **CSS selector** targets HTML elements for styling.
```css
.class, #id, tag { property: value; }
```

An **event** fires when user interaction or browser activity occurs.
```
click, keydown, load, submit, resize
```
The **Document Object Model** represents the HTML page as a tree of nodes.

The **viewport** is the visible area of a web page in the browser window.
```html
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width">
```
**Responsive design** adapts layouts to different screen sizes using media queries and flexible units.

**Flexbox** is a CSS layout model for distributing space along a single axis.
```css
display: flex; justify-content: center;
```

**CSS Grid** is a two-dimensional layout system.
```css
display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 2fr;
```

A **pseudo-class** selects elements in a specific state.
```css
a:hover, input:focus, li:nth-child(2)
```

CSS **custom properties** (variables) store reusable values.
```css
:root { --color: blue; } .el { color: var(--color); }
```
**Specificity** determines which CSS rule applies when multiple rules target the same element. Inline > ID > Class > Tag.
